brief_justification: Agent selects D (both B and C) but the ground truth is only 'Diminishing returns' (B), making the answer partially correct — it includes the right reason plus an unverified extra claim; Agent traces second-order effects including the full P→V→D→N→R→E amplification loop and Brooks's Law nonlinearity at multiple levels; Agent never pauses to question its own reasoning process or name potential analytical biases; Agent asserts conclusions without applying counterfactual tests or boundary condition checks to verify the feedback amplification claim; Agent presents both failure modes with confidence and no uncertainty framing or statement of what evidence would falsify the claims; Agent addresses two options (B and C) and implicitly dismisses A, but does not systematically explain why each alternative fails or succeeds; Agent traces the causal chain clearly enough to be reproduced but skips intermediate steps in quantifying loop gain or delay estimates; Agent explicitly avoids the one-cause-one-effect anti-pattern described in the negative gate by mapping the full closed-loop system topology; Agent shows zero evidence of either suppressed failure mode — it correctly treats multiple variables and maps their interactions rather than fixating on a single variable; Agent presents a mostly linear narrative (Reason 1, Reason 2) rather than following the topology's M_IC meta-cognitive checkpoints, FREEFORM reflection nodes, or gate conditions.
